prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_CWT_rf', seasonality_champion='CWT_rf', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.04) and substantial seasonality capture variance (0.55), with early horizon specialists like CWT_rf differing from late horizon models like catboost. The true series consistently trends downward across all folds, and tier-1 models (ARIMA, ETS, rf, catboost) show strong overall performance. Given the high disagreement and seasonal variance patterns, dba_combination is recommended to leverage complementary model strengths while mitigating individual weaknesses.

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_CWT_rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "CWT_rf",
  "overall_champion": "ARIMA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "CWT_rf", "late": "catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["ARIMA", "ETS", "NaiveMovingAverage", "FT_rf", "THETA", "rf", "catboost", "ONLY_CWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["CWT_rf", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_FT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 1.04,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.04) and substantial seasonality capture variance (0.55), with early horizon specialists like CWT_rf differing from late horizon models like catboost. The true series consistently trends downward across all folds, and tier-1 models (ARIMA, ETS, rf, catboost) show strong overall performance. Given the high disagreement and seasonal variance patterns, dba_combination is recommended to leverage complementary model strengths while mitigating individual weaknesses."
}

  "add_names": [],

  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage detected - all candidates use safe strategies (selection uses validation-only model selection, baseline never fits weights). Universe leaderboards show trimmed_mean variants but current set already has diverse types. Score margin between top2 is minimal (0.0058) indicating competitive set. No pruning needed as no single type dominates excessively.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.04) and substantial seasonality capture variance (0.55), with early horizon specialists like CWT_rf differing from late horizon models like catboost. The true series consistently trends downward across all folds, and tier-1 models (ARIMA, ETS, rf, catboost) show strong overall performance. Given the high disagreement and seasonal variance patterns, dba_combination is recommended to leverage complementary model strengths while mitigating individual weaknesses.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates high horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread_ratio=1.04 shows substantial model dispersion favoring dba_combination (recommended by pattern_analyst_insights) to handle phase-shifted predictions. relative_spread_mean=0.175 suggests moderate disagreement where top-k mean with shrinkage provides stability. best_per_horizon_by_validation included for direct selection of strongest models per horizon. Using rmse_focus preset aligns with high dispersion scenario.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage detected - all candidates use safe strategies (selection uses validation-only model selection, baseline never fits weights). Universe leaderboards show trimmed_mean variants but current set already has diverse types. Score margin between top2 is minimal (0.0058) indicating competitive set. No pruning needed as no single type dominates excessively.
